items.title: Trump accused of running ‘misogynistic administration’ after Bondi dismissal
items.standfirst: Bondi and Kristi Noem the only two cabinet members to be removed despite string of scandals involving male officialsDonald Trump has been accused of running a “misogynistic administration” after making Pam Bondi the second woman to be fired from a cabinet already dominated by men.The US president dismissed the attorney general on Thursday amid mounting frustration with her performance, especially over the release of files on convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Continue reading...
items.content: Bondi and Kristi Noem the only two cabinet members to be removed despite string of scandals involving male officialsDonald Trump has been accused of running a “misogynistic administration” after making Pam Bondi the second woman to be fired from a cabinet already dominated by men.The US president dismissed the attorney general on Thursday amid mounting frustration with her performance, especially over the release of files on convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump fires Pam Bondi as US attorney general
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump ousted Attorney General Pam Bondi on ​Thursday after mounting frustration with her performance, especially over the release of files on late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Trump also felt Bondi was not moving quickly enough to prosecute critics and adversaries whom he wanted to face criminal charges, according to sources.
items.content: US President Donald Trump ousted Attorney General Pam Bondi on ​Thursday after mounting frustration with her performance, especially over the release of files on late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Trump also felt Bondi was not moving quickly enough to prosecute critics and adversaries whom he wanted to face criminal charges, according to sources.

items.title: Trump sacks attorney general, replaces with ex-personal lawyer
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday and named his former personal lawyer to serve as the acting chief of the Justice Department. The move, which the Republican president announced in a social media post, comes amid criticism of Bondi's handling of the Epstein files and her failure to successfully prosecute several perceived Trump political foes. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.
items.content: US President Donald Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday and named his former personal lawyer to serve as the acting chief of the Justice Department. The move, which the Republican president announced in a social media post, comes amid criticism of Bondi's handling of the Epstein files and her failure to successfully prosecute several perceived Trump political foes. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

clusters.title: Myanmar’s parliament elects coup-leading general as civilian president
clusters.neutral_headline: Myanmar's coup leader who set off a civil war becomes president
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Sky News World and others

items.title: Myanmar’s parliament elects coup-leading general as civilian president
items.standfirst: Myanmar's pro-army parliament on Friday elected Senior General Min Aung Hlaing as president days after he relinquished his top military post, as required by the constitution, and passed it to the country's former spymaster. Min Aung Hlaing, the leader of the 2021 coup, was elected after an electoral process dismissed as civilian window dressing by monitoring groups.
items.content: Myanmar's pro-army parliament on Friday elected Senior General Min Aung Hlaing as president days after he relinquished his top military post, as required by the constitution, and passed it to the country's former spymaster. Min Aung Hlaing, the leader of the 2021 coup, was elected after an electoral process dismissed as civilian window dressing by monitoring groups.

clusters.title: Hegseth ousts US Army chief of staff
clusters.neutral_headline: Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US army
bullet_summary: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has asked General Randy George to step down as chief of staff of the US Army, an official said Thursday
The official, who spoke on condition of anonymity, confirmed a report from US broadcaster CBS that said George had been asked to take immediate retirement
One of the sources said Hegseth wants someone in the role who will implement President Trump and Hegseth's vision for the Army

items.title: Hegseth ousts US Army chief of staff
items.standfirst: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has asked General Randy George to step down as chief of staff of the US Army, an official said Thursday. The official, who spoke on condition of anonymity, confirmed a report from US broadcaster CBS that said George had been asked to take immediate retirement. The reason for the request was not immediately known, but CBS quoted a source as saying Hegseth wanted someone who would implement his and President Donald Trump's vision for the Army.
items.content: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has asked General Randy George to step down as chief of staff of the US Army, an official said Thursday. The official, who spoke on condition of anonymity, confirmed a report from US broadcaster CBS that said George had been asked to take immediate retirement. The reason for the request was not immediately known, but CBS quoted a source as saying Hegseth wanted someone who would implement his and President Donald Trump's vision for the Army.

items.title: Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US army?
items.standfirst: He will replace Randy George, who was asked to step down from his post by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.
items.content: He will replace Randy George, who was asked to step down from his post by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.

items.standfirst: Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster.Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022, toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. items.title: Why did U.S. Embassy urge Americans to leave Iraq? #politics
items.standfirst: The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad issued an urgent security alert telling Americans in Iraq to leave immediately, warning of possible terrorism in Baghdad in the following two days. The alert was triggered by reports that Iran aligned terrorist groups may be preparing attacks. Embassy guidance focused…
items.content: The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad issued an urgent security alert telling Americans in Iraq to leave immediately, warning of possible terrorism in Baghdad in the following two days. The alert was triggered by reports that Iran aligned terrorist groups may be preparing attacks. Embassy guidance focused…

items.title: Americans in Iraq warned to leave 'now' amid threats of imminent attacks
items.standfirst: The U.S. Embassy in Iraq has warned Americans to leave the country as soon as possible over threats from militias aligned with Iran to carry out imminent attacks in Baghdad over the next two days."U.S.
items.content: The U.S. Embassy in Iraq has warned Americans to leave the country as soon as possible over threats from militias aligned with Iran to carry out imminent attacks in Baghdad over the next two days."U.S.
